overview.description: The United States Natural Gas Fund LP (UNG) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Front Month Natural Gas index. The fund holds near-month futures contracts in natural gas, as well as swap contracts. UNG was launched on Apr 18, 2007 and is issued by Marygold.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Pull Back in Early Trading
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were lower following three sessions of gains with the market weighing the weather outlook and the pick-up in LNG exports against ample storage and expectations of higher produ...

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Start Week Lower
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were lower with the market more focused on the loss of weather-driven demand than the U.S.-Iran agreement to end the Middle East conflict.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Steady in Early Trading
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were steady with a milder near-term weather outlook partly compensated by recovering LNG feedgas flows.

data.title: Natural Gas and Oil Forecast: Oil Breakdown Deepens as Iran-Israel Conflict Lingers — NatGas Steady?
data.text: Ceasefire stability amid Iran-Israel tensions allows energy prices to consolidate on fundamentals amid strong US production and healthy storage. WTI faces downside risk below $88, Brent retests channe...

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Retreat Ahead of Storage Data
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were lower ahead of the EIA's weekly inventory report with the market continuing to move in tandem with shifts in the weather outlook.

data.title: US natural gas futures rise as demand outlook strengthens
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures climbed about ​3% on Wednesday on forecasts for hotter-than-normal weather and more demand over the next two weeks than previously expected.

data.title: US natgas output and demand to hit record highs in 2026, EIA says
data.text: U.S. natural gas supply and demand will both rise to record highs in ​2026, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said in ‌its Short-Term Energy Outlook on Tuesday.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Inch Down in Early Trading
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were modestly lower as a cooler weather outlook for the second half of June crimped demand expectations.

data.title: Natural U.S. Gas Futures Decline on Weather Outlook
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures lost ground on lower temperature forecasts for the second half of June and higher production.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Give Back Some Gains
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were lower after advancing the previous two sessions on a hotter weather outlook and the report of a below-average weekly storage injection.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Rise on Weather Outlook
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures gain with warmer weather forecasts lifting demand expectations for coming weeks.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Tread Water
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were slightly lower with the market awaiting a recovery in LNG feedgas flows after maintenance and higher weather-driven demand into the weekend and next week.

data.title: US natural gas futures fall as LNG export flows hit four-month low
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures slid about ​2% on Tuesday as daily flows to liquefied natural gas (LNG) export plants dropped to a four-month low.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Return Some Gains
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures pulled back from overnight highs while maintaining support from higher temperature forecasts moving into June.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Edge Down
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures edged lower as the Nymex July contract moved to the front of the curve.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Steady Ahead of Storage Data
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were holding around the key $3 mark ahead of the EIA's weekly inventory report due at 10:30 a.m. ET.

data.title: US LNG export plant gas flows set to hit 16-week low despite expected return of Golden Pass
data.text: The amount of natural gas flowing to the nine big U.S. liquefied natural gas ‌export plants looked set to hit a 16-week low on Tuesday even though QatarEnergy/Exxon Mobil's Golden Pass plant in Texas ...

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Head for Weekly Gains
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were higher and on track to post weekly gains, supported in part by a warmer weather outlook and a close-to-normal storage report for last week.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Gain Ground
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were higher, fluctuating from one day to the next as the market weighs fading heating demand against a gradual pickup in cooling needs.

data.title: US natgas output to hit record high in 2026, while demand declines, EIA says
data.text: U.S. natural gas output will rise to a record high in 2026, while demand ​will decline, the U.S. Energy Information Administration said ‌in its Short-Term Energy Outlook on Tuesday.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Extend Decline
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures fell, with oil markets continuing to sell off on peace deal hopes and domestic weather-driven demand seen light for the next couple of weeks.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Lower in Early Trading
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were lower as oil prices fell on a report the U.S. and Iran were close to a deal.

data.title: Morgan Stanley sees US gasoline inventories falling to historic lows
data.text: U.S. gasoline inventories are drawing down sharply, heading toward historical summer lows, as a collapse in imports and shifts in refinery yield tighten supply, ​Morgan Stanley said in a Monday note.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Start Week Higher
data.text: U.S. natural gas futures were are gaining as mixed weather patterns offered some support with cooling demand in the south and lingering heating demand in the north

data.title: Where could gas prices be by Memorial Day?
